title: galium viscosum var. campestre
intro: Galium viscosum var. campestre, also known as sticky bedstraw, is a variety of the Galium viscosum plant, a type of flowering plant in the bedstraw family. Traditionally, sticky bedstraw has been used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of sticky bedstraw have been associated with various health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Sticky bedstraw can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
